RAW MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
July 14,
1995
Peter Leigh Snell
Public Affairs Support
Services,
Inc.
401 Wythe Street,
Suite 2-A
Alexandria, Virginia 22314
Re:
Your Request for. Informal
Assistance
Our File No.
1-95-218
Dear Mr.
Snell:
This is in response to your letter requesting advice on
behalf of the Travelers Group Inc.
concerning the campaign
dIsclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the
“Act”) 1
Since you are.requesting general advice we are treating your
request as one for informal assistance.
4
You are writing on behalf of Public Affairs Support Services
(“PASS”)
which provides administrative services to the Travelers
Group Inc.
(“Travelers”)
in connection with its separate
segregated fund the Travelers Group Inc. Political Action
Committee
(“T1AVPAC”).
You indicated that
TRAVPAC is organized
pursuant to the Federal Election Campaign Act and was formed and
exists primarily to support political campaigns at the Federal
level of government.
You additionally indicated that it is
registered with and operated pursuant to the regulations
promulgated and administered by the Federal Election Commission
(“FEC”).
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000,
g.
All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2
..
.
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114;
2 Cal.
Code of Regs.
Sections
18329(c) (3) .)
428 3 STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


Snell,
Peter Leigh
No.
1-95-218
Page
2
Travelers has now decided to establish a non-Federal
political action committee for the purpose of supporting
California state and local candidates, ballot measures and/or
committees to becalled the Travelers Group Inc. Political Action
Committee
- California
(“TRAVPAC-California”).
outJines several operating wL reporting
procedures which will guide the operation of TRAVPAC-California.
We have reviewed those procedures and they are in compliance with
California law and regulations.
In addition,
you should be aware
that under the Act,
TRAVPAC may qualify as an intermediary because
it is transferring contributions it receives into the •account of
TRAVPAC-California.
For disclosure purposes, if a transfer of $100 or. more from
one contributor is made to TRAVPAC-California, it must indicate on
its campaign statement
(Form 450 or 420)
that
TRAVPAC is acting as
an intermediary for that contributor.
We are enclosing for your reference Regulation 18421.1 which
indicates when a contribution is “received”.
The regulation
provides disclosure standards for committees that receive
contributions by means of membership dues or payroll deductions.
Additionally,
you should be aware that Travelers,
the
sponsoring entity of TRAVPAC-California,
may incur separate filing
obligations if it has sufficient qualifying activity on its own.
However,
Regulation 18419(c)
provides that the sponsor will not
qualify as a committee or incur any separate filing obligations so
long as:
(1)
The sponsor does not make or receive a
ufficient amount of contributions or independent
expenditures, other than those in support af its
sponsored committee, to satisfy the thresholds set foith
in Government Code Section 82013...
(2)
The sponsored committee reports all
contributions and expenditures made in support of the.
committee by -the sponsor, its intermediate units,
and
the membets of such entities..:
-
(3)
The sponsored committee reports as an
intermediary the sponsor and, if required by paragraph
(f)
of this regulation,
any intermediate unit,
as an
intermediary, if the sponsor or intermediate unit
directly or indirectly provides the committee with $100
or more in member contributions regardless of whether
any member for whom the sponsor or intermediate unit
acts contributed $100 or more;
and
(4)
A responsible officer of the sponsor,
as well
as the treasurer of the sponsored committee, verifies


Snell, Peter Leigh
No.
1-95-218
Page
3
the committee’s campaign statement pursuant to
Government
Code Section 81004.
(Emphasis added.)
If you have additional questions, please contact me at
(916)
322-5662.
Sincerely,
Wayne
.
Imberi
Political Reform Consultant

sections.question_synthetic: What are the campaign disclosure requirements and operating procedures for establishing a non-Federal political action committee (TRAVPAC-California) to support California state and local candidates and measures, and what intermediary disclosure obligations and sponsor filing requirements apply?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC reviewed TRAVPAC-California's proposed operating and reporting procedures and found them compliant with California law. TRAVPAC may qualify as an intermediary requiring disclosure on Form 450 or 420 when transferring $100+ from contributors. The sponsor (Travelers) can avoid separate filing obligations if four conditions are met: (1) sponsor doesn't independently satisfy contribution/expenditure thresholds, (2) sponsored committee reports all sponsor contributions/expenditures, (3) sponsored committee reports sponsor/intermediate units as intermediaries if providing $100+ in member contributions, and (4) responsible officers verify campaign statements.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is informal advice letter (noted as 'informal assistance' which does not provide immunity). No explicit QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections with headers, but question and conclusion content are present in body text. File No. 1-95-218 matches document ID. Response letter dated July 14, 1995.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the campaign disclosure requirements and operating procedures for establishing a non-Federal political action committee (TRAVPAC-California) to support California state and local candidates and measures, and what intermediary disclosure obligations and sponsor filing requirements apply?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC reviewed TRAVPAC-California's proposed operating and reporting procedures and found them compliant with California law. TRAVPAC may qualify as an intermediary requiring disclosure on Form 450 or 420 when transferring $100+ from contributors. The sponsor (Travelers) can avoid separate filing obligations if four conditions are met: (1) sponsor doesn't independently satisfy contribution/expenditure thresholds, (2) sponsored committee reports all sponsor contributions/expenditures, (3) sponsored committee reports sponsor/intermediate units as intermediaries if providing $100+ in member contributions, and (4) responsible officers verify campaign statements.

embedding.summary: The FPPC provides informal advice to Travelers Group regarding compliance requirements for establishing TRAVPAC-California, a state-level political action committee. The FPPC confirms the proposed procedures comply with California law and outlines intermediary disclosure obligations and sponsor filing exemption conditions.
